support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Get help, find answers, or submit a ticket
support.faq1A: You earn 50% recurring commission on every membership your referrals pay — every month they stay active. You also earn from the 8-tier campaign grid (40% direct + 6.25% across 8 uni-level tiers) and course sales. Share your referral link to get started.
support.faq2A: Watch & Earn is the engine that powers the campaign tier grid. When you watch videos, you deliver real views to other members' campaign tiers. This keeps their campaigns active and their commission qualification alive. It is not a standalone earning feature — it is the mechanism that makes the entire affiliate commission structure work.
support.faq3A: Each campaign tier has a grid of 64 positions across 8 levels. When members purchase a tier, they fill positions in their sponsor's grid. Commissions flow at each level. When a grid completes (64 members), the owner receives a completion bonus (up to $3,200) and a new grid opens automatically. You can repurchase tiers to reactivate your qualification and keep earning.
support.faq4A: Withdrawals are paid in USDT on the Polygon blockchain. You need to complete KYC verification (one-time) and have 2FA enabled. Minimum withdrawal is $10. Go to Wallet and request a payout.
support.faq5Q: What's the difference between Basic and Pro?
support.faq5A: Basic ($20/mo) gives you access to the core platform: referral commissions, campaign grid, Watch & Earn, LinkHub, Link Tools, and the Marketing Suite. Pro ($35/mo) adds SuperPages, AI Funnels, email autoresponder, CRM, and the leads dashboard.
support.faq6A: Share your personal referral link: www.superadpro.com/join/[your-username]. You can find it on your dashboard, in Social Share, or generate a QR code for it in the QR Generator. Anyone who signs up through your link becomes your direct referral.

cryptoGuide.title: Crypto Wallet Guide
cryptoGuide.subtitle: Set up your wallet in 5 minutes
cryptoGuide.heroSubtitle: Even if you have never used crypto before
cryptoGuide.heroDesc: SuperAdPro uses cryptocurrency for payments and commissions. This guide walks you through setting up a free wallet in about 5 minutes. No technical knowledge required.
cryptoGuide.step1P1: MetaMask is a free crypto wallet used by over 100 million people worldwide. It works as a browser extension on desktop and as a mobile app on iOS and Android.
cryptoGuide.step1P2: Go to metamask.io and download the official app. Create a new wallet, set a strong password, and you will be given a 12-word recovery phrase.
cryptoGuide.step1Warning: Write your 12-word recovery phrase on paper and store it somewhere safe. Never share these words with anyone — they are the only way to recover your wallet if you lose access.
cryptoGuide.step2P1: SuperAdPro uses the Polygon network because transactions cost less than 1 cent, compared to $5-15 on Ethereum. This means more of your money stays in your pocket.
cryptoGuide.step2P2: In MetaMask, tap the network dropdown at the top of the screen and select "Polygon Mainnet". It is usually pre-loaded — just select it. If you do not see it, tap "Add Network" and search for Polygon.
cryptoGuide.step3P1: Your wallet address is like your bank account number — it is safe to share. In MetaMask, tap your account name at the top to copy your address. It starts with "0x" and is 42 characters long.
cryptoGuide.step3P2: You will need this address to receive commissions from SuperAdPro and to add it to your account settings.
cryptoGuide.step3Tip: Go to your Account page on SuperAdPro and paste your wallet address in the Crypto Wallet section. This is where your commissions will be paid.
cryptoGuide.step4P1: USDT (Tether) is a stablecoin — its value stays at $1. You use USDT to pay for memberships and campaign tiers on SuperAdPro.
cryptoGuide.step4P2: You can buy USDT on any major exchange like Binance, Kraken, or Coinbase. Once purchased, withdraw the USDT to your MetaMask wallet address.
cryptoGuide.step4Warning: Always select "Polygon" as the withdrawal network when sending USDT to your MetaMask. Selecting the wrong network (like Ethereum) means your funds will arrive on a different network and may not be accessible.
cryptoGuide.step4Tip: You will also need approximately $0.50 worth of POL (Polygon's native token) for transaction fees. This small amount is enough for hundreds of transactions.
cryptoGuide.step5P1: Your MetaMask wallet is now set up on Polygon with USDT. You can pay for your SuperAdPro membership, activate campaign tiers, and receive commission payments — all directly to your wallet.
cryptoGuide.step5P2: No payment processor can freeze your funds. No banks involved. Your money, your control. Commissions are paid directly to your wallet address.
cryptoGuide.securityDesc: No payment processor can freeze your funds. Commissions pay out directly to your wallet. No banks, no middlemen, no delays.

help.title: SuperLeads Help
help.subtitle: Complete guide to your CRM & autoresponder

items.title: What is SuperLeads?
items.desc: SuperLeads is your built-in CRM and email autoresponder. It lets you capture leads from your SuperPages funnels, organise them into lists, send automatic email sequences, and broadcast one-off emails — all without needing a separate email marketing tool.

items.title: How do leads get into my CRM?
items.desc: Leads are added automatically when someone fills out a form on any of your SuperPages. Their name and email are captured instantly and appear in your Leads tab. You can also import leads manually using a CSV file via the Import tab.

items.title: Who can use SuperLeads?
items.desc: SuperLeads is a Pro member feature. You get up to 5,000 leads included with your Pro membership. Need more capacity? You can expand at any time from your account settings.

items.title: What happens when a lead comes in?
items.desc: When a lead fills out your funnel form, they are added to your CRM, assigned to your most recent active email sequence, and the first email in that sequence is sent immediately via Brevo. Everything is automatic — no manual steps needed.

items.title: What does the Leads tab show?
items.desc: The Leads tab is your main contact list. It shows every lead you have captured or imported, including their name, email, which list they belong to, their current status, how many emails they have received and opened, and which sequence they are assigned to.

items.title: Lead Statuses Explained
items.desc: New — just added, no interaction yet. Nurturing — in an active email sequence. Hot — automatically flagged when a lead opens 2+ emails or clicks any link. Converted — manually mark a lead as converted once they have taken the action you wanted (purchased, signed up, etc.).

items.title: What is a Hot Lead?
items.desc: A Hot Lead is someone showing real interest — they have opened multiple emails or clicked a link. These are your warmest prospects and worth prioritising. Hot leads are automatically flagged with a fire icon and appear when you click the Hot filter.

items.title: Filtering your leads
items.desc: Use the coloured filter buttons to view leads by status: All, New, Nurturing, Hot, or Converted. Use the All Lists dropdown on the right to filter by a specific list. Combine both filters — for example, view only Hot leads from your Facebook list.

items.title: Assigning a sequence to a lead
items.desc: In the Sequence column next to each lead, use the dropdown to assign or change which email sequence they are in. The next time you click Send Next on that sequence, it will include this lead.

items.title: Deleting a lead
items.desc: Click the red trash icon on the right of any lead row to delete them. This removes the lead and all their email history permanently. Use with care — this cannot be undone.

items.title: What are Lists?
items.desc: Lists let you organise your leads into groups. For example: Facebook Leads, YouTube Traffic, Webinar Signups, or VIP Customers. Each list has its own colour so you can spot them instantly in your leads table.

items.title: Creating a List
items.desc: Click New List, give it a name, an optional description, choose a colour, and optionally assign a default email sequence. Any lead imported into or captured for this list will automatically be enrolled in that sequence.

items.title: Default Sequence on a List
items.desc: When you set a default sequence on a list, every new lead added to that list (via form capture or CSV import) is automatically enrolled in that sequence. This saves you from manually assigning sequences to every new lead.

items.title: What happens if I delete a List?
items.desc: If you delete a list, all leads that were in it are moved to Unsorted rather than being deleted. You can filter for Unsorted leads and reassign them to a new list at any time.

items.title: Lead count on List cards
items.desc: Each list card shows a lead count so you can see at a glance how many contacts are in each group. The coloured bar at the top of each card matches the colour you chose for that list.

items.title: What is an Email Sequence?
items.desc: A sequence is a series of emails sent automatically over time. You write them once, set the delay between each email, and the system sends them to your leads on autopilot. Perfect for welcome series, nurture campaigns, or onboarding flows.

items.title: Creating a Sequence
items.desc: Click Create Sequence, give it a name, and add your emails. Each email needs a subject line, a body (written with the rich text editor), and a send delay in days. Day 0 sends immediately. Day 3 sends 3 days after the previous email.

items.title: What does Send Next do?
items.desc: Clicking Send Next on a sequence sends the next pending email to every lead assigned to that sequence. The system tracks which email each lead is up to, so each person gets the right email at the right step — nobody gets the same email twice.

items.title: Send delay explained
items.desc: The delay on each email is the number of days after the previous email was sent. Email 1 at 0 days sends immediately. Email 2 at 2 days sends 2 days after Email 1. Email 3 at 5 days sends 5 days after Email 2. Leads who are not yet due for their next email are skipped.

items.title: Editing a Sequence
items.desc: Click Edit on any sequence card to change the name, add or remove emails, update subjects and bodies, or adjust delays. Changes apply to future sends — leads who have already received an email will not receive it again.

items.title: Rich Text Editor
items.desc: The email body editor supports bold, italic, underline, bullet lists, numbered lists, headings, and links. Write your email as you want it to appear. Emails are sent as HTML so formatting is preserved in the inbox.

items.title: What is a Broadcast?
items.desc: A broadcast is a one-off email sent to all or a filtered group of your leads right now. Unlike sequences (which drip over time), a broadcast goes out immediately to everyone matching your filter. Use it for announcements, promotions, or time-sensitive news.

items.title: Filtering your broadcast recipients
items.desc: Before sending, choose a List and/or a Status filter. The recipient count updates live so you know exactly how many people will receive the email before you hit Send. For example: send only to Hot leads on your Facebook list.

items.title: Recipient count
items.desc: The number shown next to the filters is the live count of leads who match your selection and are not unsubscribed. This is the exact number of emails that will be sent when you click Send.

items.title: Unsubscribed leads
items.desc: Leads who have unsubscribed are automatically excluded from all broadcasts and sequence sends. You do not need to filter them out manually — the system handles this for you to keep you compliant.

items.title: Broadcast vs Sequence
items.desc: Use a Sequence for planned, evergreen email series (welcome flows, follow-ups). Use a Broadcast for one-time sends to your whole list or a segment — flash sales, webinar invites, product launches, or important updates.

items.title: Importing leads via CSV
items.desc: Go to the Import tab, upload a CSV file or paste CSV text directly. Your CSV should have columns for email and optionally name. Click Preview to check the data before importing, then click Import to add the leads to your CRM.

items.title: CSV format
items.desc: The simplest format is two columns: email and name. For example: email,name on the first row, then john@example.com,John Smith on each subsequent row. The importer is flexible — it will detect the email column automatically even if your headers are different.

items.title: Assigning to a List on import
items.desc: Before importing, select which list you want to import leads into using the Into List dropdown. Choosing a list means all imported leads are immediately organised and can benefit from that list's default sequence.

items.title: Preview before importing
items.desc: Click Preview to see how many leads were detected in your CSV and check for any formatting issues. The count shown is how many leads will actually be imported. Only click Import once you are happy with the preview.

items.title: Lead limit
items.desc: Your current lead count and limit are shown at the top of the Import tab. Pro members can hold up to 5,000 leads. If you are approaching your limit, purchase an Email Boost pack or contact support to discuss expanding your plan.

items.title: Duplicate handling
items.desc: If an email address already exists in your CRM, the import will skip that lead rather than creating a duplicate. Your existing lead data (status, sequence assignment, email history) is preserved.

items.title: What is Email Boost?
items.desc: Email Boost lets you send more emails than your daily free allowance. Every Pro member gets 200 free emails per day. If you need to send more — for a big broadcast or a large sequence — you can purchase Boost Packs that give you extra credits on top of your daily allowance.

items.title: How the daily limit works
items.desc: You get 200 free emails every day. The counter resets at midnight UTC. If you send a broadcast to 150 leads and have 3 sequences going, your free 200 emails are used up first. Once they run out, Boost credits kick in automatically.

items.title: Boost Packs available
items.desc: 1,000 emails — $5. 5,000 emails — $19. 10,000 emails — $29. 50,000 emails — $99. Boost credits never expire — they roll over indefinitely until used.

items.title: How credits are deducted
items.desc: Free daily credits are always used first. Once your 200 free emails for the day are spent, each additional email automatically deducts one Boost credit. You never need to switch between them manually — it all happens in the background.

items.title: Purchasing a Boost Pack
items.desc: Click Buy on any Boost Pack card. The cost is deducted from your SuperAdPro wallet balance. Credits are added instantly — you can use them straight away. Make sure your wallet has enough funds before purchasing.

items.title: Checking your usage
items.desc: The progress bar at the top of the SuperLeads page shows your daily email usage (e.g. Daily: 45/200). The Credits counter in the top-right of the hero shows your current Boost credit balance. Both update in real time.

items.title: When do I need Boost credits?
items.desc: If your list is small (under 200 leads) and you are sending one broadcast per day, you will rarely need Boost credits. They become useful when you have large lists, run multiple sequences simultaneously, or want to send several broadcasts in a single day.

publicPages.noListingsInCategory: No listings in this category yet.

publicPages.termsContent1: By using SuperAdPro, you agree to these terms. SuperAdPro is a video advertising and affiliate marketing platform. Members earn commissions by referring others and participating in campaign grids.
publicPages.earningsDisclaimerContent: Participation in the platform involves financial risk and should be treated as a business venture. Past performance of other members is not indicative of your own results.
publicPages.membershipCommContent: Members earn 50% commission when a referred member activates a membership. However, commissions are capped at the referring member's own tier level. A Basic member ($20/mo) earns a maximum of $10 per referral, regardless of the product purchased. A Pro member ($35/mo) earns up to $17.50 per referral. This applies to both initial signups and monthly renewals.
publicPages.upgradePolicyContent: Members upgrading from Basic to Pro pay only the $15 difference between tiers. The upgrade fee goes 100% to the platform and does not generate a commission for the referring sponsor. From the next billing cycle onwards, the member renews at the Pro rate ($35/mo) and the sponsor earns their tier-capped commission on each renewal.
publicPages.campaignTierCommContent: Campaign tier purchases are split as follows: 40% to the direct sponsor, 50% distributed across 8 uni-level tiers (6.25% each), 5% to the grid completion bonus pool, and 5% to the platform. Commission qualification requires an active membership and completion of daily Watch to Earn requirements.
publicPages.campaignViewContent: Each campaign tier includes a set number of video views delivered by the Watch & Earn community. Once the view target has been achieved, the campaign completes. Members may repurchase the same tier to reactivate their commission qualification and submit a new video ad. Grids reactivate automatically upon completion — when a grid reaches 64 members, the completion bonus is paid and a new grid opens. This creates a long-term recurring income opportunity through ongoing repurchase and grid reactivation.
publicPages.courseCommContent: Course sales follow a 100% pass-up system. Sales 1, 3, 5, and 7 are kept by the seller. Sales 2, 4, 6, and 8 pass up to the sponsor in the commission chain.
publicPages.platformRightsContent: SuperAdPro reserves the right to modify, adjust, or update the following at any time, with or without prior notice, in order to maintain the integrity, fairness, and sustainability of the platform:
publicPages.rightsViewTargets: Campaign view targets — the number of views required per campaign tier may be adjusted based on community size and ad-delivery capacity.
publicPages.rightsCommRates: Commission rates and structures — including but not limited to referral percentages, grid commission splits, pass-up patterns, and qualification requirements.
publicPages.rightsMembership: Membership pricing and tier features — including the addition, removal, or modification of platform tools and capabilities included in each tier.
publicPages.rightsPolicies: Platform policies and operational rules — including but not limited to acceptable use policies, withdrawal thresholds, KYC requirements, and content moderation standards.
publicPages.rightsNotice: Any significant changes to commission structures or campaign view targets will be communicated via email and platform notification at least 7 days in advance.
publicPages.privacyContent1: We collect the minimum data necessary to operate the platform - your name, email, and payment information. We use Brevo for email and Cloudflare for security.
publicPages.privacyContent2: We do not sell your data to third parties. We use industry-standard encryption for all sensitive data including KYC documents.
publicPages.acceptableUseContent: Members may not use SuperAdPro to promote illegal products or services, distribute malware, harvest user data, or engage in deceptive marketing practices. Violation may result in account termination.
publicPages.cryptoPaymentsContent: All earnings are paid in USDT on the Polygon blockchain. Cryptocurrency transactions are irreversible. SuperAdPro is not responsible for losses due to incorrect wallet addresses or network errors.
publicPages.kycIntro: SuperAdPro is required to verify the identity of members before processing withdrawals. This helps prevent fraud, comply with international anti-money laundering regulations, and protect the community.
publicPages.kycDocuments: Full legal name, date of birth, and a government-issued photo ID - passport, driving licence, or national ID card.
publicPages.kycUseContent: Your documents are used solely to verify your identity and comply with regulatory requirements. They are never shared with other members or third parties.
publicPages.kycStoreContent: Documents are encrypted and stored securely in Cloudflare R2 object storage. Access is restricted to authorised administrators only.
publicPages.kycRightsContent: You have the right to request access to your personal data, request corrections, or request deletion of your KYC documents at any time by contacting support.
publicPages.kycProcessingContent: KYC applications are typically reviewed within 24-48 hours. You will be notified via email once your verification is approved or if additional documents are required.
publicPages.amlContent1: SuperAdPro is committed to preventing money laundering and the financing of terrorism. We implement appropriate controls to detect and report suspicious activity.
publicPages.amlContent2: We monitor transactions for suspicious activity and reserve the right to freeze accounts, refuse withdrawals, and report suspicious activity to relevant authorities.
publicPages.amlContent3: Members are prohibited from using SuperAdPro to launder funds, obscure the origins of funds, or conduct transactions on behalf of sanctioned individuals or entities.
publicPages.readyToAdvertiseDesc: Create a free account and launch your first campaign today.
publicPages.faqA1: Yes — registering is completely free. You pay for membership ($20/mo Basic or $35/mo Pro) to activate your earning ability and access the platform features.
publicPages.faqA2: Commissions are credited to your platform wallet instantly when they're earned. You can withdraw to your crypto wallet at any time, subject to a minimum withdrawal threshold.
publicPages.faqQ3: What's the difference between Basic and Pro?
publicPages.faqA3: Basic ($20/mo) gives you full affiliate earnings, campaign grid access, and your affiliate link. Pro ($35/mo) adds SuperPages builder, SuperLeads CRM, email autoresponder, lead analytics, and AI content tools.
publicPages.faqQ4: Do I have to recruit people to earn?
publicPages.faqA4: Not exclusively. While referring others accelerates income through membership commissions, you also earn grid completion bonuses driven by your whole team's activity — not just people you personally recruited.
publicPages.faqQ5: How does the campaign grid work?
publicPages.faqA5: Each campaign tier has an 8×8 grid with 64 positions. As members across your network join that tier, positions fill. When all 64 fill, a completion bonus is triggered and paid to the grid owner. A single campaign can complete multiple times.
publicPages.faqA6: When anyone in your network (up to 8 levels deep) makes a grid purchase, you earn 6.25% of that purchase automatically. This means a large team generates passive income for you without any additional effort.
publicPages.faqQ7: What currency are earnings paid in?
publicPages.faqA7: All earnings are in USDT (a dollar-pegged stablecoin) paid on the Base blockchain. You need a compatible crypto wallet to receive withdrawals.
publicPages.faqA8: When you sell a course, you earn a direct commission. Every 3rd sale passes up to your sponsor — who earns a larger commission. This creates a powerful incentive chain.
publicPages.faqQ9: Is there a lock-in period for membership?
publicPages.faqA9: No. Membership is billed monthly with no contract. You can cancel at any time, though your earnings will stop when membership lapses.
publicPages.faqA10: Members can access support through the platform's help system. You can also reach us through the contact page.
publicPages.advertiserDesc: SuperAdPro members watch videos as part of earning campaigns. Your ads get genuine attention from motivated viewers.
publicPages.genuineViewsDesc: Members are incentivised to watch fully — no bots, no skipping, no fake engagement.

publicPages.costEffectiveDesc: Competitive CPM rates with guaranteed view delivery across our member network.
publicPages.recurringExposureDesc: Members return daily to watch campaigns — your brand gets repeated exposure.

help.title: SuperPages Help
help.subtitle: Knowledge base for the page builder

items.title: How the Editor Works
items.desc: SuperPages is a free-form drag-and-drop page builder. Click any block from the right panel to add it to your canvas, then drag to position it anywhere. Double-click text elements to edit inline. Use the floating toolbar to format text, change fonts, colours and alignment.

items.title: Selecting & Moving
items.desc: Click any element once to select it — you'll see a blue outline and resize handles appear. Click and drag to reposition. Hold Shift + Arrow keys to nudge by 10px, or Arrow keys alone for 1px precision.

items.title: Resizing
items.desc: Selected elements show 8 cyan resize handles — on all four corners and four edges. Drag any handle to resize. Corner handles resize in both directions simultaneously. Minimum size is 40px wide by 20px tall.

items.title: Saving & Publishing
items.desc: Click Save to save your work (auto-saves every 30 seconds). Click Publish to make your page live on the web. Your live URL appears in Settings. Toggle back to Draft to unpublish.

items.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
items.desc: Ctrl+S: Save | Ctrl+Z: Undo | Ctrl+Y: Redo | Delete/Backspace: Remove selected element | Arrow keys: Nudge 1px | Shift+Arrows: Nudge 10px | Escape: Deselect

items.title: Heading
items.desc: Large, bold text for page titles and section headers. Default is 36px Sora font in white. Double-click to edit text. Use the floating toolbar to change font family, size, colour, bold, italic, underline, and alignment.

items.title: Text
items.desc: Body text for paragraphs and descriptions. Default is 15px Outfit font in grey with 1.8 line height for readability. Double-click to edit. Supports all inline formatting from the floating toolbar.

items.title: Label
items.desc: Small badge-style text perfect for tags like PREMIUM, NEW, or LIMITED OFFER. Comes with a rounded pill background and border. Double-click to change the label text.

items.title: Image
items.desc: Add images to your page. Click the edit IMAGE button in the element toolbar to enter an image URL or upload a file (max 5MB). Supports JPG, PNG, GIF and WebP. Images are stored on Cloudflare R2 for fast global delivery.

items.title: Video
items.desc: Embed videos from YouTube, Vimeo, or upload your own MP4/WebM files (max 50MB). Click edit VIDEO to add. YouTube and Vimeo watch URLs are automatically converted to embed format. Uploaded videos are hosted on Cloudflare R2.

items.title: Audio
items.desc: Add audio players to your page. Click edit AUDIO to enter an audio URL or upload MP3/WAV/OGG files (max 20MB). Great for podcasts, music samples, or audio testimonials.

items.title: Button
items.desc: Clickable button with gradient background. Click edit LINK to set the destination URL. Double-click to change the button text. Perfect for Join Now, Buy Now, or Learn More calls to action.

items.title: Opt-In Form
items.desc: Email capture form with name field, email field, and submit button. Pre-styled with a dark glass background. Click edit FORM to customise the HTML. Connected to your My Leads dashboard for lead collection.

items.title: CTA (Call to Action)
items.desc: Similar to Button but styled specifically for primary conversion actions. Click edit LINK to set the destination URL. Double-click to edit the text. Use for your main page action like Get Started Now.

items.title: Review
items.desc: Customer review block with star rating, quote text, and attribution. Styled with a dark card background and cyan left border. Double-click to edit the review content, stars, and customer name.

items.title: Badge
items.desc: Small decorative badge identical to Label — useful for BEST VALUE, MOST POPULAR, or FEATURED tags. Has a gold pill style by default.

items.title: Testimonial
items.desc: Extended testimonial block with stars, longer quote, and author credit. Features a gold left border to distinguish from reviews. Double-click to customise the testimonial text and attribution.

items.title: FAQ
items.desc: Question and answer block styled with a subtle bordered header. Double-click to edit the question and answer text. Stack multiple FAQ elements to build a full FAQ section.

items.title: Stat
items.desc: Large statistic display — shows a big number (like 95%) with a label underneath. Perfect for social proof: 10,000+ Members, 95% Payout Rate, $1M+ Paid Out. Double-click to edit.

items.title: Progress
items.desc: Animated progress bar with label and percentage. Click edit SET to configure the label text, percentage (0-100), and bar colour. Great for showing course completion, funding goals, or limited availability.

items.title: Countdown
items.desc: Live countdown timer that ticks down to a target date and time. Click edit SET to choose the target date. Displays days, hours, minutes and seconds. Creates urgency for launches, sales deadlines, or event dates.

items.title: Socials
items.desc: Row of social media icons — YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, X/Twitter, and LinkedIn. Click edit LINKS to add your profile URLs for each platform. Icons link to your social profiles on the published page.

items.title: Icon + Text
items.desc: Feature block combining an emoji/icon with a heading and description text. Perfect for listing benefits, features, or steps. Double-click to edit the emoji, heading, and description.

items.title: Separator
items.desc: Decorative divider line with optional centre text. Double-click to change the separator text. Use between sections to create visual breaks.

items.title: Logos
items.desc: As seen in logo strip — displays brand names in a horizontal row. Double-click to edit the brand names. Use to show media mentions, partner logos, or trust signals.

items.title: Spacer
items.desc: Invisible spacing element. Drag to position and resize to control the gap between other elements. Has no visual appearance on the published page — purely for layout control.

items.title: Box
items.desc: Empty container with a subtle border and rounded corners. Use as a background card behind other elements to create grouped sections. Resize and layer behind text, images, or forms.

items.title: Divider
items.desc: Simple horizontal line. Use to separate sections on your page. Resize the width to control how far it spans across the canvas.

items.title: Embed
items.desc: Raw HTML/code embed block. Click edit CODE to paste any HTML, including iframes, custom widgets, tracking pixels, or third-party embed codes. The code renders on the published page.

items.title: Page Title
items.desc: The name of your page — appears in the browser tab, search engine results, and your SuperPages listing. Choose something descriptive and compelling for SEO.

items.title: Page URL Slug
items.desc: Customise the web address of your published page. Open Settings, find the Page URL Slug field, and type your preferred name using lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Click Save Settings to apply the change. Each slug must be unique — you cannot use one that is already taken by another page.

items.title: Meta Description
items.desc: The description that appears below your page title in Google search results. Write 150-160 characters that tell visitors what your page offers. Important for click-through rates from search.

items.title: Social Share Image
items.desc: When someone shares your published page on Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, or WhatsApp, this image appears as the preview thumbnail. Upload or enter the URL of a 1200x630px image for best results.

items.title: Page Status
items.desc: Draft pages are only visible to you. Published pages are live on the web at your Live URL. Toggle between Draft and Published at any time. Unpublishing a page makes it immediately inaccessible to visitors.

items.title: Canvas Background
items.desc: Change the page background using the colour picker or image upload in the top-right of the Blocks panel. Supports solid colours, gradients, and full-page background images uploaded to Cloudflare R2.

items.title: AI Chat
items.desc: Click AI Assistant at the bottom of the Blocks panel to open the AI chat. Tell it what you want: add a heading, change background to navy, write sales copy for a fitness product. The AI can modify your page style and generate content suggestions.
